results.title: Ticketing Insights Analyst II – Career Management Center Colorado State University
highlights:
# Ticketing Insights Analyst II
...
EchoStar is reimagining the future of connectivity. Our business reach spans satellite television service, live-streaming and on-demand programming, smart
...
installation services, mobile
...
and products.
...
This role serves as the analytical backbone of the Wireless Ticketing Insights team, converting complex, unstructured ticketing data into prioritized, quantified problem statements that drive strategic business decisions. Operating within a rigorous six-step pipeline—Detect, Size, Diagnose, Route, Validate, and Report—this position diagnoses root causes of operational friction and routes validated insights to cross-functional resolution teams. The primary challenge involves translating fragmented data and operational “noise” into clear, actionable narratives that proactively prevent customer pain and streamline ticket processes. By leveraging cutting-edge AI and machine learning capabilities, the role architects automated, preventative solutions that move the department beyond simple manual reporting.
...
- Move the team away from manual data handling by developing automated workflows and predictive models; identify and build the business case for AI-augmented analytics (like LLM-driven workflows) to improve speed-to-insight
...
Strong skills in querying large datasets, investigating raw records, and transforming unstructured or noisy operational data into analysis-ready outputs using advanced SQL.
...
Expertise in leveraging modern cloud-based analytics platforms to build automated visualizations, surface insights, and develop compelling data-driven business cases.
...
Demonstrated capability in translating complex artificial intelligence and machine learning concepts, including agentic AI and LLM workflows, into actionable intelligence for non-technical leadership.
...
Advanced AI literacy, including practical experience incorporating knowledge-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and prompt engineering into analytical workflows.
...
Minimum Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or a related quantitative discipline (or 2+ years of equivalent practical experience)
...
Minimum Experience: 2 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or operational reporting
...
Cloud-based analytics platforms
...
AI-assisted analytics tools or machine learning applications
...
- Proven capability to translate complex AI/ML concepts into actionable business intelligence for non-technical stakeholders and senior leadership (Director/Head-of level)
...
Programming experience in Python or an equivalent scripting language to build advanced analytical models is strongly preferred.

Developing intelligent persuasive conversational agents to change people's opinions and actions for social good is the frontier in advancing the ethical development of automated dialogue systems. To do so, the first step

intricate organization of strategic disclosures and appeals employed in human persuasion conversations. We designed an online persuasion task where
...
participant was asked to persuade the other to donate to a specific charity. We collected a large dataset with 1,017 dialogues and annotated emerging persuasion strategies from a subset. Based
...
the annotation, we built a baseline classifier with context information and sentence-level features to predict the 10 persuasion strategies used in
...

...
strategies led to a
...
The emergence of chatbots, along with the development of artificial intelligence, has led to a growing interest in developing conversational agents to effectively interact with people. So far, the primary goal of these agents is to facilitate task-completion and engagement in fields such as healthcare, e-commerce, and fundraising. While persuasive technologies for behavior change have successfully leveraged other system features, the development of artificial persuasive agents remains lagged due to a lack of synergy between social scientific research on persuasion and the computational development of conversational systems. In this abstract, we introduce the foundation work towards building an automated personalized persuasive conversational system. We first collected 1000 conversations on human-human persuasion conversations about charity donation. We designed a persuasive conversation strategy annotation scheme and annotated the collected conversations. Then we analyzed the relations among participants’ personality traits and moral foundation values and their donation behaviors and analyzed the moderating effects of these psychological factors on different persuasive strategies. Finally, we briefly discuss the current endeavor in building a multi-label multi-task machine learning model to automatically classify and generate personalized persuasive strategies. Show less

Recently, I realized a pain point in how I learn with large language model assistants: the conversation is linear.
...
If I don’t understand multiple concepts in the AI’s response, I’m forced to ask them one by one, which creates long nested threads. This breaks my flow, makes it harder to track where I was, and pulls me away
...
I was trying
...
So I built a Knowledge Graph Learning Assistant — a project that transforms AI conversations into an interactive graph of nodes and edges.
...
You can branch off into sub-nodes when you need a quick detour
- You can jump around freely without losing your place
...
- And you can always return to
...
main thread with one click
...
It also includes a lightweight helper search tool: when you hit an unfamiliar term and don’t want to deep-dive, you can instantly look it up like a smart glossary.
...
This project is my attempt to reshape how AI-assisted learning works — not as a long scroll of text, but as a flexible, visual knowledge network that matches how many of us actually think.
...
If you’re someone who likes visual learning, nonlinear exploration, or simply wants a calmer way to study with AI, I’d love to share more or get your feedback.

As a Data Science and Engineering Manager at DISH Network, I lead a team of data scientists and engineers to create and deploy AI/ML solutions for various business domains, such as marketing, ad tech, hardware engineering, and AIOps. Some of the projects I have worked on include market mix modeling, dynamic creative optimization, multi-touch attribution, predictive network models, and predictive budget allocation. I have 7 years of experience in data science, having previously worked as a lead data scientist at Evalueserve and a senior data scientist at Mu Sigma. I have successfully delivered data-driven solutions that generated measurable business value for clients across industries, such as petcare, retail, and telecom. For example, I led a team of 6 analysts in a $1.3MM engagement to build a sales force planning and optimization tool that increased sales uplift by 3% for a Russian petcare manufacturer. I hold a Bachelor of Engineering degree from Bangalore Institute of Technology, and I have completed multiple certifications in neural networks and deep learning from Coursera. I am proficient in Amazon Web Services, machine learning, and statistics. I am passionate about leveraging AI/ML to create business value and solve real-world problems. results.title: HR Generalist – Career Management Center Colorado State University
highlights:
# HR Generalist
...
EchoStar builds solutions that help families and communities stay connected. We’ll launch your career and empower you to change lives.
...
EchoStar is reimagining the future of connectivity, and our Human Resources team is the engine driving this transformation. We are seeking energetic, detail-oriented early-career professionals to join our team across three critical functional tracks. This unified requisition allows you to apply for a foundational role where you will contribute to high-impact projects—from launching enterprise-wide talent strategies to optimizing the digital employee experience.
...
The HR Generalist role is foundational to ensuring the delivery of efficient HR processes. You will provide day-to-day support for employees and people managers, executing consistent delivery of HR support related to the entire employee lifecycle.
...
Key Responsibilities:
...
- Employee Relations: Provide HR consulting and coaching to help business leaders solve personnel issues and drive organizational culture.
- Lifecycle Management: Support cyclical strategic activities such as performance appraisals, talent planning, and employee experience survey action planning.
- Process Improvement: Initiate projects focused on the continuous improvement of HR processes to ensure a frictionless employee experience.
- Regulatory Expertise: Provide expertise in technical and regulatory aspects of employment, including hiring, performance management, and rewards.
...
- Education: Bachelor’s degree preferred.
- Experience: 0-1+ years of Human Resources experience across multiple disciplines.
- Interpersonal Skills: Proven ability to build strong relationships and act as a trusted partner to employees and managers.
- Adaptability: Ability to handle multiple competing priorities in a fast-paced, results-driven environment.
...
What Success Looks Like
...
- Impact: Successful execution of early-career recruiting projects and timely delivery of HR technology enhancements.
- Experience: Delivery of a frictionless, positive experience for both candidates and internal employees.
- Growth: Developing subject matter expertise across multiple HR disciplines while acting as a brand ambassador for EchoStar’s culture and values.
...
Compensation: $27.00/Hour – $32.00/Hour Benefits
...
The posting will be active for a minimum of 3 days. The active posting will continue to extend by 3 days until the position is filled.

results.title: Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
...
# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.

results.title: Senior AI Research Scientist job at Slingshot Aerospace in US National | Wfh
highlights:
Senior AI Research Scientist job at Slingshot Aerospace in US National | Wfh
...
# Senior AI Research Scientist job at Slingshot Aerospace in US National
...
Title: Senior AI Research Scientist
...
In this role, you will join the AI and Data Science department within Slingshot's Research and Development (R&D) organization. You will contribute directly to Slingshot's vision to accelerate space sustainability and create a safer, more connected world. You will identify opportunities for AI to contribute to Slingshot's mission goals by pioneering novel algorithms that leverage diverse data streams and advanced intelligence engines and by integrating the advancements into broader AI systems across the Slingshot technology platform.
...
- Research and design AI systems, models, and advanced machine learning algorithms to augment physics-driven modeling and simulation systems and enable intelligent AI agents to reason, plan, and adapt based on simulated and real-world sensor data
...
- Lead innovation in AI-driven simulation tooling, identifying opportunities to enhance workflows through reinforcement learning, multi-agent systems, and hybrid modeling approaches
...
- Collaborate with research, engineering, and product teams to build AI-driven solutions that meet mission-critical modeling and decision-support needs
...
- AI/ML expertise
- Demonstrable experience in the application of sophisticated AI/ML methodologies to science and/or engineering disciplines, including deep learning, generative models (e.g. LLMs, diffusion models), agentic systems, reinforcement learning, computer vision, or other emerging areas of AI research
- Hands-on experience developing and deploying supervised and/or unsupervised learning models
...
- Passion for Space and AI/ML applications
...
- Experience with fine-tuning LLMs, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and domain adaptation for scientific/engineering datasets using modern ML frameworks and model hubs
...
- Familiarity with Reinforcement Learning (RL) and multi-agent reinforcement learning to enable training agents that learn strategies in simulation and real-world contexts
...
- Strong understanding of neural networks, transformer architectures, attention mechanisms, and optimization methods to extend or fine-tune transformer-based models
...
- Experience building reusable internal tools (connectors, simulation frameworks, evaluation harnesses) to refine simulation-based datasets for AI agent training in support of research, data-driven insights/analytics, and model development
...
- Peer-reviewed publications and/or presentations in a scientific discipline, AI/ML focus preferred
...
- Demonstrable combined experience indicative of skillsets required to utilize APIs, microservices, and workflows that merge physics simulation engines with AI training pipelines
...
- Familiarity with common agentic protocols (MCP, A2A, etc...)
...
- Practical working experience with physics simulations, Monte Carlo methods, probabilistic modeling, and Bayesian methods
...
- 6+years industry or commensurate academic research experience (e.g. PhD and/or MS) in scientific or AI/ML-related domains
...
- Knowledge of parallelization, GPU acceleration, and performance optimization for simulations and training workloads.
...
### Location: Remote, US
